page_content: embarking on a career in law enforcement is not just a profession; it is a solemn commitment to protect and serve, ensuring the safety and wellbeing of the communities we are sworn to safeguard. as you step into the challenging and noble realm of policing, the significance of effective police station management and crime control cannot be overstated.

the police officers have to carry out their duties and responsibilities with due respect to the acts, rules and regulations passed by the legislature and courts. further the police administration has to change from person centric to system centric administration.

this comprehensive manual is more than a guide - it is your compass, pointing you towards the principles and practices that underpin our collective mission to uphold justice and maintain public order. the complexities of modern society demand a nuanced approach to law enforcement, and this book is designed to be your ally in navigating the intricate landscape of police work.

police work is not just a job; it is a calling. it requires dedication, integrity, and a profound understanding of the delicate balance between order and justice. this book is your companion on this journey, equipping you with the knowledge and skills necessary to meet the evolving demands of our dynamic society.

i extend my deepest appreciation to sri g. venkata rao, ad, tspa for his dedication in compiling the book with the help of sri v. bhaskar, dsp, tspa, under the guidance of smt. c. anasuya, ips, dy. director induction training & course director to induction training of sctsis( civil).
[ abhilasha bisht, ips ] director, tspa
hyderabad

metadata.heading: police station - a public facility (citizen friendly service delivery unit)

page_content: every kind of service necessary to the public good becomes honorable by being necessary

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: police station - a public facility (citizen friendly service delivery unit)

page_content: - albert schweitzer

the foundation of the police is the police station. it ensures public order, does investigation of crimes and performs multifarious other tasks. the police station is also the primary point of communication between the citizen and the police. a police station should be clean and welcoming citizens so that they can approach without inhibitions or fear to register their complaints and grievances. it should also meet the operational and welfare needs of the personnel stationed there.

according to sec. 2(s) of cr.p.c. - police station means any post or place declared generally or specially by the state government, to be a police station, and includes any local area specified by the state government in this behalf.

police station is a public facility which functions round the clock like a hospital. a police station is kept open 24 hours because anyone in need of its services at any point of time, day or night, can count on its services (24x7) for 365 days.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: characteristics of citizen- friendly police station

page_content: police station as a 'citizen-friendly, citizen-centered service delivery unit' refers to a facility open to the public that allows individuals to develop trust and rely on a sense of safety and security, hassle-free living, and citizen dignity. the term 'public' refers to all people, regardless of caste, creed, gender, or social standing. the term 'facility' refers to something that facilitates, assists, or comes to the aid of the general population. a police station is a public building, which signifies that the police is a government agency dedicated to serving the public and assisting them in times of need.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: following essentials reflect the spirit of 'citizen friendly, citizencentered service delivery unit:

page_content: 1. free accessibility 2. reception 3. registration of cases 4. human touch 5. rendering help 6. petition management services

as people freely and confidently walk up to a hospital, a post-office or a bank to get their legitimate work done, so should the victims, complainants or aggrieved people, be able to walk up to a police station without any restriction/hesitation. if a victim or a complainant is afraid of going to a police station, it negates the very purpose of sanctity of a police station. somehow over the years we have created an impression that people are not welcome at the police station and those who come would, be ill-treated. in some stations, the police station staff scares away even people who are innocent and legitimately pass by the path- ways around the police station. this has to be changed by deliberate efforts on our part. that people should be allowed free access and should be courteously treated.

all police stations now have a reception or helpdesk, a suitable place to which the visitors are automatically guided by duty woman constable or sentry police constable. every police station has a furnished and earmarked, computerized, reception with trained women staff as receptionist. a visitor's book is also kept in the reception, to take feedback from the public.

100% registration of all complaints and prompt response is an important function. constitution bench of the supreme court in lalita kumari vs government of u.p. and others on 12-11-2013 had addressed the issue of mandatory registration of firs and had held that registration of fir either on the basis of the information furnished by the informant when it makes out a cognizable offence u/s 154(1) of the cr, p.c. or otherwise u/s 157 (i) of the cr. p.c. is obligatory. it also held that reliability, genuineness and credibility of the information are not conditions precedent for registering a case u/s 154 of the cr. p.c. the supreme court has provided some exceptions to the mandatory registration of fir and for the conduct of preliminary inquiry. therefore, to ensure accountability in the functioning of police, that all actions of the police should be reduced to writing and documented through entries made in the general diary of the police station.

since most of the visitors to the police station are aggrieved persons or victims, they are under emotional stress. they expect the policeman to understand their plight and give emotional support by way of empathy and sympathy. it is again a common complaint that instead of getting empathy, most often police treatment further aggravates the emotional strain, making them repent as to why they had come to the police station. human touch in the dealings of the police with people would go a long way in making people feel that the police station is a public facility and a citizen friendly service delivery unit.

police station staff shall guide a victim/complainant to a relief they are looking for rather than simply point them in the right direction. it is also important to be smart and sensible. they must have the ability to evaluate and understand their surroundings at all times, and anticipate what's ahead. this enables them to think quickly, especially in the face of crisis times. proper guidance helps them achieve their goals of policing. policeman is trained not only in law and policing skills but also in first aid, emergency relief, fire-fighting, swimming etc., again for the purpose that the police should help the people in times of emergencies.

providing the public transparent as well as an accountable policing services in all means, where the citizen will be provided with definite acknowledgement for each and every representation/petition they make. it provides the opportunity for the complainant know about the activities of investigation undergoing.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: administrative structure of police station at district level:-

page_content: at district level, the superintendent of police is the head of the district and he is assisted with addl. superintendents of police administration and ar depending on the sanctioned posts. the sp will be assisted by the sdpos of the rank of asp/dsp, each in charge of a subdivision. a sub division is further divided into circles headed by inspectors of police. each circle is sub-divided into police stations under a subinspector who is called the station house officer with assistant subinspectors, head constables and constables and wherever necessary with additional sub-inspectors to assist him. some police stations have outposts and check-posts attached to them and each is manned by asis/hcs with constabulary assistance. they are located wherever necessary for the prevention and reporting of crime. there are other wings like special branch, task force, district crime records bureau, district training centre, police control room also function to assist the district police (sp)

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: administrative structure of police station at district level:-

page_content: the basic functional unit of police is the police station, which is called station house as notified by the government. these police stations are classified as law and order police stations, detective police stations, traffic and highway police stations, women police stations and railway police stations. each of the above categories are primarily meant for certain specific police functions.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: administrative structure of police station at district level:-

page_content: in all 'a' grade municipal towns and commissionerates there will be separate police stations for law and order, crime investigation and traffic, for efficient police functioning. where a separate police station for crime investigation and traffic cannot be established, these duties will be performed by the law and order police station with additional strength. these police stations as far as possible will be headed by inspectors of police as station house officers, assisted by sub-inspectors, asst. sub-inspectors, head constables and constables.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: administrative structure of police station in commissionerates:

page_content: the cities of hyderabad, cyberabad, rachakonda, warangal, karimnagar, khammam, siddipet, nizamabad and ramagundam are headed by commissioners of police who are of the rank of addl. dgp/igp/dig. the structure and functions of the police in these commissionerates is similar to that of hyderabad city.

metadata.heading: administrative structure of police station in commissionerates:

page_content: the commissioner of police, hyderabad city is assisted by addl. csp/jt.csp, and dy. commissioners of police in charge of, law and order, crimes, traffic, special branch, administration, coordination and armed reserve and commandant, sar cpl.

metadata.heading: administrative structure of police station in commissionerates:

page_content: for the purpose of law and order, the city is divided into zones, each under the charge of dy. commissioner of police (dcp) of the rank of superintendent of police. dcp is assisted by addl. dcp. each zone is subdivided into divisions headed by an officer of the rank of asst. commissioner of police (acp). sub-divisions are further divided into police stations under the charge of an inspector assisted by sub-inspectors, assistant subinspectors, head constables and constables.

the police in a democratic polity perform multiple and complex tasks within the ambit of law. towards this objective, the police have to be an effective organization for the prevention, detection and investigation of crime, maintenance of law and order, protection of life, liberty, and property of the people, to bring offenders to justice and to render honest and impartial service to the people. however the changing internal security scenario has added a new dimension to the task of the police and brought the police force closer to the center-stage of governance. consequently, it is an imperative to secure a police organization that is structurally cohesive, functionally competent to fulfill the wide-ranging goals of the organization and provide efficient as well as qualitative services to the people. in performing this role, the police must maintain highest standards of integrity, professionalism while acting within the framework of the constitution and laws of the land.
1. the police force of the telangana state is under the general control and supervision of director general of police, appointed by the state government.
office of the director general of police, which is also called as chief office or dgp office, consists of -

1. police headquarters with state control and communication complex. 2. organization and legal wing. 3. personnel wing. 4. law and order wing. 5. technical wing. 6. recruitment wing. 7. railways & road safety wing. 8. coordination, welfare, home guards & sports wing. 9. p & l wing. 10. training wing.
the director general of police (dgp) is assisted by:

1. addl. dgp (law & order) 2. addl. dgp (personnel) 3. addl. dgp (organization and legal) 4. addl. dgp (railways and road safety) 5. addl. dgp (coordination, welfare, home guards & sports) 6. addl. dgp (crime investigation department) 7. addl. dgp (intelligence and security) 8. addl. dgp (armed police) 9. addl. dgp (technical services) 10. chairman, t.s. level police recruitment board (tslprb). 11. addl. dgp (women security, she teams and bharosa), hyderabad. 12. addl.dgp operations (greyhounds & octopus) 13. igp (p & l)

14. igp (training) 15. igp multi zone-i 16. igp multi zone-ii 17. director, it & c. 18. addl.dgp operations (greyhounds & octopus) 19. director, t.s. forensic science laboratory (tsfsl) 20. director, t.s. cyber security bureau (tscsb) 21. director, t.s. anti narcotic bureau (tsanb) 22. director, t.s. level command control centre 23. commissioner of police, hyderabad. 24. commissioner of police, cyberabad 25. commissioner of police, rachakonda 2. the state is divided into two (2) multi zones and seven (7) zones, each headed by a ig/digp. the cities of hyderabad, cyberabad, rachakonda, warangal, khammam, ramagundam, karimnagar, nizamabad and siddipet are headed by the commissioners of police. the addl. dgp (l&o) will have supervision over the zonal isg/disgp and commissioners of police except that of hyderabad city besides supervision over chief public relations officer, whose office is located in the chief office. 3. the addl. dgp (organization & legal) main functions are to effectively organize the police establishments and their reorganization effectively where necessary and to take up the matters with the govt. for sanctions and is assisted by sp(legal) regular monitoring of disposals of court cases in non-service and service matters, filing counters on behalf of the dgp, ensuring prompt assistance to legal officers, government pleaders and prosecutors without any delay in the office administration of the chief office. 4. the addl. dgp (personnel) is assisted by aig (admn) in the office administration of the chief office. there is one chief accounts officer to assist the igp p&l. the dgp printing press will be under the charge of a technical officer of the rank of dsp as manager borrowed from the government printing press or recruited. the dgp press shall be under the administrative control of addl.dgp (personnel). 5. the addl. dgp cid is assisted by (1) igp /dig pcr (2) igp/dig/sp admn., (3) igp/dig/sp economic offences (4) igp/sp gender offences (5) igp/sp scrb assisted by director, finger print bureau (fpb) and (6) 4 ssp/disgp one each for organized crime & narcotic cell; property

- offences; pcr & social justice and human rights. there is also a legal branch under him for rendering legal assistance in criminal cases. cid is also declared as police station by the state government. the addl. dgp, cid ensures linkages with scrb/fpb. 6. the addl. dgp intelligence and security is assisted by 2 isgp one for intelligence and one for security. there shall be 3 more disgp, one each for general intelligence, operations and special intelligence branch (sib).

7. the addl. dgp (tssp battalions) is assisted by dig p (battalions). 8. the addl. dgp technical services is assisted by 1 dig (tech) and (1) for communications and police computer services. 9. addl. dgp recruitment and chairman, slprb is assisted by director, tspa. 10. the additional director general of police (railways and road safety) monitors railways and co-ordinates the functions of various agencies relating to road safety measures with headquarters in dgp office, hyderabad. 11. the commissioner of police, hyderabad who is of the rank of addl. dgp is assisted by addl./joint csp who in turn are assisted by dy. commissioners of police in charge of law and order, crime, traffic, administration, special branch and armed reserve and by commandant, special armed reserve - central police lines (sar-cpl) amberpet, hyderabad. 12. each zone / range in the state consists of 4 or more districts. each district is headed by a superintendent of police, assisted by two addl. ssp. the addl. sp (l&o) will also be in charge of administration and armed reserve. the other addl. sp will be in charge of district investigation squads and district crime records bureau (dcrb). in some districts depending upon the need, one more addl. sp is posted to supervise special operations. the district special branch works under the direct supervision of the superintendent of police. - a) the superintendent of police in the district is also assisted by subdivisional police officer of the rank of asp/dsp, each in charge of a sub-division. a sub-division is further divided into circles headed by inspectors of police. - b) each circle is sub-divided into police stations under a sub-inspector who is called the station house officer with assistant subinspectors, head constables and constables and wherever necessary with an additional sub-inspector to assist him. some police stations
have out-posts and check-posts attached to them and each is manned by asis / hcs with constabulary assistance. they are located wherever necessary for the prevention and reporting of crime.

13. the commissioner of police, hyderabad, is assisted by addl. csp/jt. csp, and dy. commissioners of police in charge of, law and order, crimes, traffic, special branch, administration and armed reserve and commandant, sar cpl. - a) for the purpose of law and order, the city is divided into zones, each under the charge of dy. commissioner of police (dcp) of the rank of superintendent of police. each zone is sub-divided into divisions headed by an officer of the rank of asst. commissioner of police (acp). sub-divisions are further divided into police stations under the charge of an inspector assisted by sub-inspectors, assistant sub-inspectors, head constables and constables. the dy. commissioner of police of the respective zones are responsible for law and order, maintenance of public order and peace and prevention and detection of crimes in their respective areas. - b) the investigation branch of city police is headed by a deputy commissioner of police and consists of crime records and criminal intelligence wing, juvenile bureau, modus operandi bureau, specialized investigating teams and divisional investigating teams including the clues team. addl. deputy commissioner of police/asst. commissioner of police supervises these teams, generally headed by an inspector. the city crime branch, which is known as detective department, handles all grave, sensational and white-collar crimes other than simple cases, which are dealt by the law and order pss. for the purpose of investigating the crimes, each division has a divisional detective acp, assisted by inspectors and sub-inspectors etc. the detective department also handles cases as entrusted by the commissioner of police, director general of police and the government. in addition, all important and sensational cases will also be dealt by the city detective department. - c) for the traffic control, regulation and enforcement of traffic rules, the city is divided into traffic divisions co-terminus with law and order zones, each headed by acp traffic. each division consists of a number of traffic police stations headed by an inspector assisted by sub-inspectors, assistant sub-inspectors, head constables and constables. the overall control over the traffic force in the city vests with the addl. cp, traffic. traffic offences will however be investigated by the respective law and order pss. - d) the city special branch is headed by a jt. commissioner of police
assisted by dcp/addl.dcp/asst. commissioner of police with a complement of inspectors, sub-inspectors, assistant subinspectors, head constables and constables.

- e) the city police has its own armed reserve called city armed reserve including city guards and mounted police, headed by a jt./dy. commissioner of police, headquarters and assisted by an addl. dcp and acsp, who are assisted by reserve inspectors (ri), reserve subinspectors (rsi), asst. reserve sub-inspectors (arsi), head constables (arhc) and constables (arpc). - f) a force called the special armed reserve (sar) under a commandant of the rank of sp assisted by asst. commandants of the rank of dsp with its complement of reserve inspectors, reserve sub-inspectors, asst. reserve sub-inspectors, head constables and constables is stationed at central police lines (cpl), amberpet, hyderabad. this force is intended as a stand-by in emergencies and for providing escorts for treasuries, prisoners, ballot boxes, examination question papers and other similar duties. it also provides the police personnel required for staff officers in dgp office. it is under the administrative control of the cp hyderabad. - g) the cities of cyberabad, rachakonda, warangal, khammam, ramagundam, karimnagar, nizamabad and siddipet are headed by commissioners of police who are of the rank of isg/disgp/ssp. the structure and functions of the police in these commissionerates is similar to that of hyderabad city. 4. 14 1. the crime investigation department (cid) of the state is the main investigating agency of the state consisting of investigating units and teams, the state crime records bureau and the finger print bureau. the women's protection cell, the sniffer dog squad and the bomb disposal teams also form part of crime investigation department. addl. dgp heads the crime investigation department, assisted by isgp/disgp and ssp. the investigating teams of cid are generally headed by dssp/inspectors, with sub-inspectors, head constables, scientific assistants and other specialized personnel. these teams are located either at the state hqrs., or at such places as prescribed by the government. these teams are supervised by ssp./disgp./isgp. the cid will undertake all criminal cases as entrusted by the dgp and the government besides undertaking direct investigation of specified offences. the cid shall be responsible for updating and maintenance of all crime records in the state and shall be in overall charge of criminal intelligence. cid is a nodal agency in the state for interpol.

2. there is (1) railway police district in the state under the control of addl.dgp railways who reports to the dgp through secunderabad railway police district headed by superintendent of police, who is assisted by one or more dssp each in charge of a railway police sub-division. railway police sub-divisions are divided into circles under inspectors and police stations under sub-inspectors with asst. sub-inspectors, head constables and constables. 15. the intelligence department is headed by an addl. dgp assisted by isgp, disgp, ssp., addl. ssp., dssp., inspectors, sub-inspectors and other ranks including ministerial staff. the shorthand bureau also functions as part of the intelligence department. under the control of addl.dgp, intelligence, there is a special intelligence branch (sib) and intelligence security wing (isw) under the charge of digp at each place to deal with left wing extremism and in providing security arrangements to all the protectees of telangana state as well as visiting protectees. sib keeps liaison with the officers concerned and units of other extremist affected states, dealing with the intelligence in the field of leftwing extremism and the anti-extremist operations. 16. addl. dgp armed police will be in charge of tssp battalions, and will be assisted by dig tssp. at present, there are 13 telangana special police battalions each under a commandant assisted by addl. commandant and asst. commandants. each battalion is divided into active companies, which are again sub-divided into platoons and sections. the battalions are controlled by an officer of the rank of dig. the movement and disposition of these battalions are subject to the orders of the dgp. however in grave emergencies, the commissioners of police, the zonal isg./disgp may move them in anticipation of approval of the dgp. 4. 17-1. the police transport organization (pto) serving the transport requirement of police department with its complement of technical and administrative staff is controlled by the director who is of the rank of igp. he is assisted by different ranks of technical and nontechnical police officers and other technical staff. the igp (pto) reports to the dgp. 2. the police communications branch of the state is under the control of the director of the rank of dig(tech) (non-cadre), assisted by technical persons of the rank of digp, sp, dsp, inspector, si, asi, hc and constable. the director police communications reports to the dgp through addl. dgp (technical services). 3. the police computer services is headed by an officer of the rank of sp who will report to the dgp through the addl. dgp (technical services). 7. 18-1. there is a state level police recruitment board (slprb) under addl. dgp (recruitment) who is also designated as chairman of the board.

- the board recruits officers of and below the rank of sub-inspectors and reserve sub-inspectors by conducting competitive examinations. 2. the training wing of the police department has various institutions to train officers and men. the prime institution of training is the t.s. police academy at the state headquarters, headed by the of the rank of an adgp. 3. the other training institutions are police training colleges headed by ssp /addl.ssp. every unit of police establishment shall have a small and compact training institute. all the police training colleges in the state are under the charge of igp training. 19. the duties and responsibilities of the addl. dgp (road safety) shall be mainly to advise the government on the road safety policies and implement the decisions of the state road safety council and oversee the functions of district road safety council. he also collects and analyses statistics relating to road accidents, studies and identifies the causes, recommends and monitors the implementation of measures to improve traffic conditions. it is also his duty to devise better methods of training to drivers and to monitor the working of highway patrols. - 20-1. specialized forces called 'greyhounds' and 'octopus' are under the charge of addl.dgp operations. the greyhounds is an elite specially trained force to provide for flexibility in operations and also for imparting specialized training to police personnel in jungle warfare. the octopus (organization for counter-terrorist operations) is a commando unit for anti terrorist operations. igp (operations) will function directly under dgp. 2. the home guards organization is a voluntary organization trained and administered by the police department. the addl.dgp home guards will be in charge of the entire home guards organization in the state. in the cities, the commissioners of police and in the districts, the ssp are in charge of training and deployment of home guards to various organizations that require their services. 3. t.s. forensic science laboratory (tsfsl) is headed by a director and reports to the director general of police. tsfsl provides all possible help that modern science can render in crime detection. it also tenders expert scientific opinion in criminal/legal cases. technical persons of various ranks assist director in discharge of his duties. 21. the department of prosecution of the state headed by the director of prosecution functions under the control of law department. the prosecution branch oversees the work of all prosecuting officers who conduct police cases in various criminal courts in the state. 22. the organizational chart of the department is appended to this chapter.
'be silent, when you don't have all the facts'

a police station (station house) is a building which serves to accommodate police officers and other members of staff. these buildings often contain offices and accommodation for personnel and vehicles, along with locker rooms, temporary holding cells and interview/ interrogation rooms.

a police station in india is typically under the command of an inspector/sub-inspector. in the case of a police station which is the headquarters of a police circle, the inspector's office is clearly demarcated and most likely functions from a separate building. in urban area police stations, the inspector rank officer shall act as station house officer and in rural area police stations the sub-inspector shall act as station house officer .

there are five categories of police stations, as given below, notified by the government under section 2(s) of cr.p.c. such a notification confers certain powers and responsibilities on the police in respect of the notified area.

all the police stations are responsible for all the police functions. but each police station as described above is meant for specific purposes. except in municipal towns the police stations established in all parts of the state carry out all the police functions including law and order, crime detection and investigation. but in all municipal areas and metropolitan cities separate police stations are established for law and order and crime investigation, and therefore each police station will carry out their respective functions allotted to them. likewise women police stations have specific functions given to them.

1. law and order police station 2. detective police station or central crime police station 3. traffic and highway police station 4. women police station 5. railway police station 6. cyber crime police station 7. balamithra police station 8. narcotic police station

page_content: the functioning of a law and order acts, cr.p.c, telangana state police manual and govt. from time to time. law and order police stations are responsible for all the police functions like prevention prosecution of offences, maintenance of law law and order police stations are set out in the police a state police manual and govt.orders and circulars aw and order police stations are responsible for all the prevention and detection of crime, investigation and maintenance of law & order. are set out in the police orders and circulars aw and order police stations are responsible for all the crime, investigation and

- a. the important function of the l&o police station is to prevent the crime and to detect the crime. the important function of the l&o police station is to prevent the crime and to detect the crime. the important function of the l&o police station is to prevent the - b. the sho of the ps shall collect the information regard to all the activities of the bad characters in the jurisdiction and keep constant surveillance over them. the sho of the ps shall collect the information well regard to all the activities of the bad characters in the jurisdiction and keep constant surveillance over them. well-in advance in regard to all the activities of the bad characters in the jurisdiction - c. preventive measures shall be taken to prevent the crime both bodily preventive measures shall be taken to prevent the crime both bodily preventive measures shall be taken to prevent the crime both bodily
and property.

- d. blue colts and patrols shall be organised properly. - e. clues shall be collected properly from the crime scene, witnesses and criminals by using required techniques and skills to detect the offences. - f. all the accused shall be arrested without abnormal delay following procedure to arrest. - g. public awareness camps shall be conducted to prevent the crime in all aspects. - h. community programmes shall be organised to get information and support from the public in prevention of the crime - i. records shall be maintained properly about the crime and criminals. liaison shall be maintained with all stakeholders in the jurisdiction.
the law and order police stations are responsible for the maintenance of the law and order in the jurisdiction.

- a. organising of proper bandobusth to maintain law and order in the jurisdiction and anti-maoists/terror operations, dispersal of unlawful assemblies and prevention of breach of peace and public order. - b. collection of information about activities in the field of communal, caste, political, student, or other segments of society including subversive elements and to keep surveillance. - c. identifying of trouble spots and trouble mongers and to take effective preventive measures like launching security proceedings under cr.p.c, etc. - d. collection of information on all important matters relevant to maintenance of law & order in the jurisdiction, police duties and functions. - e. maintenance of effective surveillance over anti-social elements and rowdies of the area. - f. maintenance of records properly pertains to law & order issues and rowdies.

the law and order police stations are responsible for the investigation of all cases reported in the jurisdiction, except for those where the investigation is entrusted to inspector/dsp or other senior officers or cid. at all law and order police stations the following duties relating to investigation and prosecution of cases shall be done.

- a. prompt registration of fir on receiving of information of cognizable in nature and to take up investigation. furnishing a copy to the complainant invariably at free of cost and send the original fir to the courts concerned immediately and copies to the superior officers. - b. in respect of non-cognizable offences reported in the station fir shall be registered as per the procedure laid down in sec. 155 cr.p.c, and to take up investigation. - c. the io/sho shall promptly visit scene of crime and thoroughly examine the same for clues and for other matters of investigation and make arrangements for the preservation of the scene if necessary. - d. the sho/io shall examine the complainant, victim, eye-witnesses and other circumstantial witnesses in detail and to record their statements properly. - e. the sho / io shall collect all documentary evidence and certificates related to the case. - f. sho /io has to identify, collect, preserve, and to forward the material objects, to scientific experts with letter of advice for examination and report. - g. the sho/io shall arrest the accused where necessary observing the necessary provisions of law and the decisions of the court. - h. to record the confession of the accused and to seize the crime weapon. - i. to produce the arrested accused before the court along with remand report within 24 hours of the arrest. - j. sho /io shall hold inquest where necessary and forward the body for the post mortem examination. - k. the sho / io shall produce the material objects seized from the accused before the court. - l. the investigating officers of law and order ps shall finalise the cases without delay and file charge sheets where the evidence is sufficient for prosecution. - m. the sho shall have to produce the witnesses before the court at the time of the trial of the case. - n. the sho shall serve the summons and the warrants issued by the court. - o. the sho shall liaise with the prosecuting officers and see that cases
should be convicted.

outposts are opened either due to remoteness of the police station, easy accessibility to the public and for convenience of performance of police duties in the area. they are attached to a police station whether in town or in a rural area and shall work under the sho of a police station. the beats in an outpost can be generally 2 to 3 and will be a part of main police station to which the outpost is attached. the beats in the outposts should be serially numbered along with the beats of the police station.
two models of duties for an out post with a strength of one hc and seven constables is given below. the alphabets stand for each constable and the duty hours are fixed at 48 hours a week.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: model chart of duties in an outpost

page_content: the outposts perform all duties of a police station except registration of cases. they are therefore responsible for effective area policing. it is not advisable to have more than 2 beats in an outpost. model no. 1 makes available at any given time 3 police officers for night duty. model no. 2 makes available 2 police officers at any time during day and night. both the models exclude the hc who will be in charge of the outpost. model 2 is advised where presence and accessibility of police at the outpost is considered necessary. model 1 is ideal for ensuring night patrolling by 2 beat area constables in one or the other 2 beats to which they stand allotted. the strength in the outpost should also be divided between the two beats or the only beat which may be formed. they shall perform all duties that beat area constables are required to perform. the officer in charge of the out post is also required to maintain the records in the out post as in the police station.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: model chart of duties in an outpost

page_content: 1. the outpost pattern should be adopted for as many beats as possible. in city and large towns as also in rural areas where there may be no need for a full fledged police station particularly in view of the communication facilities available. in appropriate cases asi or a sub-inspector can head the out post and the strength increased if the beats are more than 2 but not exceeding 3. 2. the outposts are provided with the communication and other facilities, which are available to a police station. the transport that may be made available should be 2 wheelers at the rate of 2 or 3 for outposts. all records as in a police station should be maintained in an outpost except fir index. a register of all cases reported and referred to pss should be maintained as indicated above.

some law & order police stations may notify as balamithra police station, which would consist a instructor, doctor and lawyer for educating the children in various fields and solve their problems apart from attending regular duties. 'balamithra' police station equipped indoor games, a sand pit, spacious hall, jungle gym, swing and slide, and other play equipments were aimed at making children comfortable in the police station precincts. pave the way for better relations between children and the police and remove bad precedent on the police system. balamithra police stations are functioning in warangal city and ramagundam commissionerates in telangana state.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: detective police station and central crime police station:

page_content: detective police stations are established in all 'a' grade municipalities and in commissionerates. these police stations are meant to detect, investigate and prosecute all offences other than connected to law and order and traffic. these offences include both conventional as well as sophisticated crimes connected to properties and other securities. in these periods of modern and advanced technology computer crimes, economic offences, speculative and money laundering offences are well dealt with under special laws enacted besides the conventional provisions of cheating, forgery and criminal breach of trust provided in ipc. apart from detective police stations, central crime stations are established in all district head quarters and cities working under the direct supervision of sp/cp. except ccs police station, hyderabad no other ccs police station in the state have the power of registration of the cases. all the cases should be registered at concerned law & order police stations and on gravity of the offence the unit officer shall take decision to transfer the case to ccs for the purpose of the speedy and special investigation.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: detective police station and central crime police station:

page_content: in view of the nature of the crime dealt with by these specialised units, the scientific and other available modern equipment should be placed at their disposal for quick and correct detection of cases.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: detective police station and central crime police station:

page_content: - a) forensic science laboratory unit with all facilities (clues teams) - b) finger print unit with photographer - c) sniffer dog squad - d) investigation tool kits - e) cyber crime equipment and gadgets

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: detective police station and central crime police station:

page_content: the detective police station is headed by an inspector as sho assisted by skilled staff well versed in the detection of various categories of conventional and sophisticated crimes. the sho and his staff shall be given periodical training in all types of white collar/economic offences, including cyber crime.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: detective police station and central crime police station:

page_content: the main functions and responsibilities of detective police stations are;

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: detective police station and central crime police station:

page_content: - a) detection, investigation and prosecution of all property crimes other than ones connected to law and order and traffic. - b) taking up investigation on scientific and methodical lines. - c) maintenance and upkeep of crime, criminal information system and crime records and computerising all crime records and criminal intelligence data. - d) to employ all scientific aids available for crime detection. - e) built up special investigation skills by being equipped with innovative ways of detection of crime in advanced society.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: detective police station and central crime police station:

page_content: the detective police station need not organise beat duties except night patrolling if any to check up some bad characters. it shall have its own plan of surveillance basing on specific events or situations.
a general diary will be maintained in the dps in the same manner as prescribed for l & o police station in respect of their functions and duties the matters dealt by them.

the inspector should always keep a minimum staff for meeting emergencies out of office hours at the dps. at least one si should be available between 2000 hrs and 0800 hrs every night at the dps/ccs. when information received during the night demands immediate presence of other officers, they should be summoned. plain clothes officers suitably armed should make the security arrangements in the dps. the arrangements should be made in a manner that is unobtrusive and does not discourage a normal complainant from approaching the dps.

on wednesdays and fridays physical training classes should be conducted apart from catechism, briefing, training and exchange of information. all personnel present at headquarters should attend. they may be allowed 2 hours off to go home and return to the station.
an asst. sub-inspector shall be the station writer assisted by a hc.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: registration of firs at detective police stations

page_content: the bifurcation of the powers between the law and order and detective police station are only for the purpose of specialized investigation. therefore, wherever an offence is reported, concerned law and order police station must register the case and transfer the case to the concerned detective police station for investigation.
the officer in-charge of the dps after receiving the case file himself should proceed to the scene or depute a sub-inspector selected for the purpose along with the necessary scientific personnel.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: investigation of cases by detective police

page_content: the inspector in charge of the dps will personally investigate murders, robberies, dacoities, grave thefts and house-breakings, professional poisoning and professional kidnapping, dowry deaths, caste and communal incidents of serious nature, economic crimes, cyber crimes and organised crimes. if the inspector, for any special reason, does not himself investigate an important case, but has it investigated by a sub-inspector, such investigation by the latter will be under the personal direction of the inspector, who should make it a point to scrutinise the case diaries and countersign them as a token of having received and ensure that the investigation is proceeding on right lines. the entrusting of investigation to subordinate sis should be done only in exceptional cases.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: detective police stations/ccs - liaison with l and o police

page_content: the sho dps and the sub-inspector will be in constant touch with their counterparts in l & o police relating to the beat information, bad character and the presence or absence of habitual criminals in the area. they should preferably be in touch every morning through telephone or other means of communication.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: detective police stations/ccs - liaison with l and o police

page_content: in every detective police station or central crime station, display boards be maintained and kept up-dated by one police officer in the dps in charge of the crime records with the following information:

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: detective police stations/ccs - liaison with l and o police

page_content: - a. the staff details including the scientific personnel attached to the team and those available for duty at any given time, those off duty and on outdoor duty indicating the crime numbers in which they are engaged. the offences pending investigation, trial and the targeted date of disposal and the officers handling each case; - b. a comparative statement of all cognizable offences of the area of the dps for the previous and current years which should include cases reported and investigated by l & o police, dps and traffic police. a suitable colour or symbol may be used to indicate the jurisdiction of the l & o police station or the traffic police station which is handling the cases. - c. a comparative statement of all non-fir cases under each act for the jurisdiction of the dps. - d. the names of all wanted persons and the officers of the dps in charge of operation to apprehend them.
the following instructions should be borne in mind by the dps regarding gathering and dissemination of intelligence.

1. the dps is responsible for gathering criminal intelligence and information, it's documentation, usage and dissemination, notwithstanding the duties of the l & o police to collect and report criminal intelligence. 2. the information to be gathered is based on criminal records and the crime criminal information system and also relates to what is obtained through enquiries, observation of places and persons, surveillance, shadowing, interrogation, contacts and sources. 3. the method of collection of such information and the techniques to be adopted is a matter of skill to be acquired. guidelines are contained in the training modules prepared by the training branch, the intelligence branch and the cid. 4. the source of information should be kept confidential. the informant sheets should be maintained by the inspector only for regular informants using a code name without mentioning the actual name

- of the informant. the handling officer's name may however be indicated in the sheet. a master register should be prepared and maintained by the sp. 5. the informants and sources on various professional categories of to be specially covered are the activities of organized crimes and criminal gangs in groups and professional criminals and receivers. 6. the information received from the area police on the cases being handled by the dps should be entered regularly in the criminal records and analyzed by the ios and their assistants. the action that needs to be taken by dps itself should be initiated by themselves promptly.

1. traffic police station will be established in all commissionerate cities and 'a' grade municipalities and also in other places depending on the necessity. each police station shall be equipped with a mobile wireless vehicle for patrolling purposes. a highway patrol vehicle shall have staff consisting one ci/si, 2-3 of sis/asis, one hc and 1 or 2 constables. the recovery vans (cranes) and other equipment needed for clearing of obstructions is provided in large cities and at other important places. 2. the traffic and highway police stations will be under the control of a dsp in the districts. in large cities where number of traffic police stations is more a deputy commissioner of police heads branch. jurisdiction consisting of a highway or fixed duties in the town as the case may be will be allotted to the sis/ inspectors. 3. the staff of the traffic ps is divided into sections and each section kept in charge of si/asi/ head constable.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: functions and responsibilities of traffic police station:

page_content: the functions and responsibilities of a traffic and highway police station shall be to achieve smooth and safe flow of traffic by efficient regulation and enforcement of traffic laws and to ensure prevention of accidents.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: functions and responsibilities of traffic police station:

page_content: 1. efficient road traffic management should maintain balance in i) satisfactory circulation ii) absolute safety and iii) reasonable economics. 2. in the mechanics of traffic management, engineering, education and enforcement should operate coordinately together for making the roads safe and the passage smooth. 3. while regulation and enforcement is the major preoccupation of the traffic police, they have to liaise with the local municipal authorities,

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: functions and responsibilities of traffic police station:

page_content: urban development authorities, roads and building agencies etc for continuously updating the road surface, geometrics and accessories like signals, islands, railings, dividers, flyovers, speed breakers and underpasses.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: functions and responsibilities of traffic police station:

page_content: 4. traffic police should organize road safety education of the drivers and all the road users more important the rising generation in schools. 5. the basic duties of the traffic police personnel include: - a) manning fixed traffic points in a town or city. - b) mobile patrol to regulate traffic and clearing the road. - c) enforcement of motor vehicle act and rules and other notifications. - d) check and control of rash, dangerous and drunken driving. - e) patrolling of highways, enforcement of traffic discipline and maintenance of order on the main roads. - f) removing obstructions to traffic flows and organizing traffic diversion whenever necessary, both in the congested areas of the towns/city and on the highways. - g) communicating information to l & o police/dps of any incident or movement of criminals or suspected persons or property, which they come to know. - h) setting up barricades to check vehicles on alert from the police authorities. - i) assisting l & o police or detective police in checking for suspicious persons or properties. - j) maintenance of prescribed records. - k) monitoring of traffic on the highways on a full time basis. 6. coordination with local authorities, highway department, telephones, water works, drainage, electricity in the matter relating to free flow of traffic and its regulation, occasioned by maintenance and repair works undertaken by the concerned departments and for erection of road signals, road painting.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: organisation and supervision of traffic police stations station house officer of traffic police station

page_content: the station house officer of traffic police station should acquire full knowledge of all roads, highways and congested areas. he should also acquire knowledge of the details of the vehicles, density of traffic, movement of contraband and suspected persons, road side truck terminals, parking places, eating houses, motels etc.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: records to be maintained in the traffic police station

page_content: the traffic police station shall maintain all the records relating to personnel and management of station in the same manner as prescribed for l & o police stations like general diary, duty roster, traffic point books, petty case register, mv act cases register, challan book, small service books, process register, a map of the area and accident prone areas, the general information book etc. the mobile patrolling teams should maintain apart from the log books, a point book similar to beat book in which all matters connected with mobile patrolling and incidents are recorded.

1. the women police stations are opened with a view to pay special attention to crimes against women and more particularly harassment and cruelty against women. they are also intended to inspire confidence and provide free communication by the women victims. the other functions include assistance to police in search, arrest, custody, escort and interrogation of women prisoners or suspects. contingents of women police are provided in other police stations also with a view to perform the above functions. 2. the records, management and functions of the women police stations shall be the same as prescribed for l & o police stations. the investigation of more serious offences particularly cases of rape, dowry deaths and organized trafficking in women shall be dealt by the l & o/dps of the area. 3. the women police station shall be part of a l & o police zone in cities or sub-division in the district and shall be under the supervision of the dcp in cities or sdpo in district. 4. the sho of the women police station shall have all the functions, duties and responsibilities as sho of an l & o police station. 5. the sho shall maintain close liaison and coordination with the officials of the women and the child welfare department, nongovernmental and voluntary organizations, the officials in charge of state homes for women, and other authorities specified in the immoral traffic prevention act and the juvenile justice (care and protection of children) act, domestic violence act, protection of children from sexual offences act etc. 6. the sho of women police station should furnish the crime-criminal information directly to the dcrb and the dps. 7. all equipment and facilities including communication etc. as are provided for l&o police station are provided to the women police stations. the preventive duties in respect of offences against women

shall rest with the women police in so far as it relates to its jurisdiction. particular attention should be paid in collecting information and taking security action against persons who indulge in organized trafficking in women and children and those who are likely to commit offences of kidnapping, abduction, child marriage and demand and acceptance of dowry.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: women police stations and women protection cell

page_content: special arrangements are made for effective investigation of crimes against women and protection of women. women police stations are established to focus on this aspect and to inspire confidence. the women protection cell in cid is intended to monitor the crimes against women and take up serious cases for investigation. the safeguards and steps to be taken for protection of women and children by the police and treatment of women offenders is a matter of concern for the police. the existence of women police stations or women protection cell does not absolve the police as a whole of its duties in this respect. there is no bar either to post men police officers to women police stations or to the protection cell, except that it is necessary that more women police officers than men are posted in those units.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: duties and powers of women police officers

page_content: the duties of women police officers are the same as are assigned to all police officers, as are attached to the post to which they are posted. all instructions and obligations, code of conduct, discipline, recruitment and training procedures, as are contained in the ts police manual, are applicable to them.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: duties and powers of women police officers

page_content: the uniform rules as are applicable to men, are also applicable to women police officers except for those who complete sixteen weeks of pregnancy. in such cases separate uniform as prescribed for ips officers (women) are khaki colour saree and blouse or khaki colour salwar - kameez can be worn.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: duties and powers of women police officers

page_content: in the exercise of powers, the women police officers shall strictly observe the precautions, limitations and other procedures prescribed particularly in relation to arrests, seizures, searches and investigation, handling of agitations and remain at all times impartial, honest and dedicated in the discharge of their duties.

the railway police is a branch of the general police and hence the orders in the police manual equally apply to them. railway police is organised into 2/3 districts each headed by a superintendent of police. the addl. director general of police, railways is the head of the organization in the state and has the same position in respect of railway police. each railway police district has sub-divisions, circles, police stations and outposts and is generally organized on the lines of district police subject to such modifications and special provisions.

1. the role of the railway police is to prevent and detect crimes, maintain order, communicate intelligence on criminals, apprehend wanted persons within the railway limits. 2. the duties of railway police are:

- a) arrest, detection and investigation of cognizable offences within the jurisdiction of the railway police; - b) inquiry and report under section 132 of the indian railways act; - c) prosecution of cognizable offences under ipc, railway act and other acts wherever applicable; - d) reporting of all instances of illegal activities including fraud on the part of railway subordinates and others; - e) traveling in trains where vips and other selected officers travel to watch out for suspicious persons, - f) collection, communication of criminal intelligence, interception and arrest of wanted persons.

- a) section traffic on the platforms, in the booking offices, waiting halls, at the entrance, exit gates, and wherever specially required on emergencies by the station officials; - b) control of vehicular and other traffic in the station compound; - c) maintenance of order in standing passenger trains, prevention of overcrowding, etc.; - d) watching loaded passenger trains when standing in stations; - e) arrest of those found committing nuisances or suffering from infectious diseases and keeping the station premises clear of idlers and beggars. - f) examination of all empty carriages on arrival at terminal stations for property left behind by passengers. - g) removal of bodies of persons dying in the trains and in station premises and conveyance of sick passengers to hospital. - h) communicate information of any agitation likely to obstruct movement of trains, destruction of railway property or installations to local police and help them to maintain law and order.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: working of the railway police stations and investigation units

page_content: 1. the railway police station staff is divided into two sections, i.e. the travelling and detective staff, and the platform staff excluding those required for police station duty hc and pcs. the detective duty personnel in the police station are intended to assist the sho in the investigation of such cases, which the police station is authorized to investigate in terms of the orders below. all other duties shall be performed and records maintained in the same manner as are prescribed for law and order police stations of the district including those specifically prescribed for railway police. 2. the investigating units headed by inspector and such number of subinspectors and head constables as determined will be responsible for investigation of the cases. the duties connected with investigation of any case are the same as prescribed for the police in the ts police manual and shall be complied with into apart from the above mentioned five types of police stations there are other wings/departments in which the measures of crime prevention and investigation should take up to facilitate the police department in prevention and detection of crime and to do quality of investigation. the details are mentioned below.

in the days of heightened rowdyism, mafia and communal tensions in the city, fore fathers of city police thought that a specialized force is essential in hyderabad city to strengthen the hands of the commissioner of police and establish durable peace. as a result, some special duty teams were formed on 01-01-1981. it was reconstituted as commissioner's task force in the year 1985. subsequently, it was declared as police station on 30-08-1996. now the task force is a well developed institution which has been safeguarding the hyderabad is from all subversive elements, mafia, gangsters and all other notorious criminals. it is needless to say now that the name task force sends chills down through the spine of all wrong doers in the city.

task force is specialized in conducting raids, searches and seizures. it has the professional competence in apprehending criminals involved serious offences and also in detection of important crimes likes murders, dacoities, robberies, extortion, kidnappings, land grabbing and other organized crimes. it has an excellent track record in curbing rowdyism in hyderabad city by nabbing communal offenders and rowdy elements. it continuously hounds the isi, illegal immigrants and maoists in the city. task force plays a crucial role in all sensational and critical law and order issues. it is a quick reaction team in all sensational and major events like ganesh, moharam, bonalu and other grave law and order situations.

in hyderabad city, zone wise teams are working. each zone team headed by inspector and he is assisted by 2/3 sis, 5 hcs, 10pcs in the team. 2/3 zonal teams works under the supervision of the dcp/addl. dcp, collects intelligence about communal troublesome persons, terrorists, maoists and co-ordinates with local police, ci cell, sib, nia etc and acts upon, and also attends all important bandobusts of public gatherings, festival gatherings i.e. ramzan, ganesh immersion procession, bonalu processions, moharrum procession etc and vvip / vip movements etc.
after the formation of the telangana in other commissionerates in the state also the system of task force wing has established and functioning effectively.

except in hyderabad city, the taskforce wing /ps has no power to register the firs. all the information and seizures must report by them to the concerned law & order police stations for registration of firs and to take up further investigation.

the crime investigation department (cid) of the state is the main investigating agency of the state consisting of investigating units, teams, the state crime records bureau (scrb) and the finger print bureau (fpb). the women's protection cell, the sniffer dog squad and the bomb disposal teams also form part of crime investigation department. addl.dgp heads the crime investigation department, assisted by isgp, disgp and ssp. the investigating teams of cid are generally headed by dssp/inspectors with sub-inspectors, head constables, scientific assistants and other specialized personnel. these teams are located either at the state hq. or at such places as prescribed by the government. these teams are supervised by ssp/digsp/igsp. the cid will undertake all criminal cases as entrusted by the dgp and the government besides undertaking direct investigation of specified offences. the legal branch in cid, consisting of chief legal advisor of the rank of district and sessions judge, legal advisor and asst. legal advisors, tenders legal opinion and advice on all crime matters during investigation, trial and appeal etc. the cid shall be responsible for updating and maintenance of all crime records in the state and shall be in overall charge of criminal intelligence of telangana. for interpol (interpol).. cid is a nodal agency in the state.

cid is having the power to register firs on the complaints forwarded by the govt. / senior officers and on the direct complaints. further the cid shall take up investigation on the important cases which are transferred from the law & order or grp.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: functions of crime investigation department are:-

page_content: - a) investigation of specified cases and cases entrusted by the government and dgp. - b) maintenance, up-dating and use of crime criminal information system, crime and criminal records, planning and implementation of criminal intelligence and crime analysis to improve prevention, investigation and prosecution. - c) coordination of investigation in the state and with other states and national institutions/organisations dealing with crime investigation.

superintendents of police: the first supervisor in all cases of cid shall be superintendent of police. they will be responsible for ensuring investigation in accordance with procedure and method prescribed, as are applicable to each case. the submission of case diaries, their scrutiny, timely steps to be taken for prompt investigation of cases taken up by cid, preparation of report for legal opinion, and liaison with local police for assistance to investigation teams are some of their more important functions.

the deputy superintendents of police and inspectors of police will be investigating officers of cases entrusted to cid. both inspectors and dsp will report to sp directly. if the digp is in-charge of his squad, they will report directly to him.
experts drawn from other branches are responsible for scrutinising the documents and records and giving advice and opinion on matters of their specialisation and aid in the investigation.

the chief legal advisor and other legal advisors will be mainly rendering legal advice and opinion in all cases investigated by cid. in all cases the written opinion of the legal advisor or chief legal advisor shall be taken before any orders of disposal are passed by the concerned officer.

the cid consists of: (1) the investigation teams of conventional crimes including murder, robbery, thefts etc. general offences wing (gow), (2) the scrb which includes the criminal intelligence, crime analysis and research centre (prc) and a coordination cell, (3) protection of civil rights cell (sc/st cell), (4) anti dacoity cell, (5) finger print bureau, (6) women protection cell (dowry deaths investigation special cell), (7) financial crimes cell i.e economic offences wing (eow), (8) organized crime cell, (9) anti-drug squads, (10) terrorist investigation cell, (11) counterfeit currency, forged travel documents squad, (12) regional investigation squads, (13) police sniffer dog squads, (14) legal branch, (15) civilian expert group and (16) cyber crime cell.

the anti-corruption bureau is a specialized agency tackling the problem of corruption in various departments of the government against public servants and also private persons whoever abets the offences under the prevention of corruption act, 1988. the bureau registers cases under the provisions of this act. besides this, the bureau conducts enquiries basing on the information/petitions received from various agencies like government, vigilance commission, lokayukta etc. and also on the information/petitions received by the bureau from the public containing specific and verifiable allegations of corruption against public servants.

this bureau was established on january 2, 1961 in combined state and functions directly under the administrative control of the general administration department of telangana government. the director general, who is a senior ips officer of the rank of dgp, heads the bureau. he is assisted by director, additional director, joint directors (3 cadre sps and 3 non cadre sps ) cadre, and additional supdt. of police.

the bureau is divided into 10 ranges, in telangana. each range encompasses one district. each district is headed by one deputy supdt., of police. the bureau is also having additional sps in the head quarters. it is having legal officers to tender advice on legal matters to conduct prosecution in courts and tribunal for disciplinary proceedings.

the bureau basically enforces the provisions of the prevention of corruption act, 1988. the bureau has sue-motto powers to collect information, conduct enquiries and register cases on public servants. the bureau investigates disproportionate assets cases of criminal misconduct and misappropriation. apart from that the bureau also conducts enquiries in the form of regular enquiries, discreet enquiries and also conducts surprise checks in areas of rampant corruption.

after completion of investigation and enquiries, a final report is sent to government through the vigilance commissioner who tenders his advice to the government. depending on the merits of the case, the further course of action may be either to file charges or to drop action. if the evidence is not enough for launching prosecution in a case, the case is referred to the tribunal for disciplinary proceedings, commissioner of enquiries or to the department concerned for taking further action by dealing with the public servant on charges of misconduct, possessing disproportionate assets and violation cs (conduct) rules, 1964. for prosecution of a public servant the authority competent to remove him has to accord sanction for prosecution.

conviction in a court leads to dismissal of the convicted public servant, whereas in departmental action the public servant is awarded a major punishment or a minor punishment depending on the gravity of the delinquency under the cs (cca) rules, 1991. major punishment involves dismissal, removal, reduction in rank, withholding of increments with cumulative effect, cut or stoppage of pension for retired employees, etc.
minor punishments are withholding of increments without cumulative effect, censure, recorded warning, etc.

deals with the offences related to cyber crimes, video piracy, copy rights act and offences under information technology act. the first cyber crime police station in combined state under the administrative control of crime investigation department was formed on 23-12-2002 vide g.o.ms.no.128 home (police) department. the police station has jurisdiction of whole state and deals with cyber offences under information technology act.

page_content: cyber crime police station deals with the cases of unauthorized access and hacking, trojan attack. virus and worm attack, denial of service attacks. pornography, forgery, intellectual property rights (ipr) violations. cyber terrorism, banking/credit card related crimes. ecommerce/investment frauds, cyber stacking, identity theft, data diddling, source code theft. breach of privacy and confidentiality and other computer related crimes. e-mail related crimes. uk job frauds, phishing, lottery frauds. investment frauds, conference frauds. seed frauds, drug peddling etc. digital investigation with forensic activity will be done at digital investigation lab. procures data from internet service providers, mobile service providers, banks, payment gateways, webmail service providers, social network service providers is done as per the provisions of sec.91 crpc.
