1. special casual leave connected with sterilization, recanalization under family welfare programme may be suffixed as well as prefixed to regular leave or casual leave, but not both. this intervening holidays and/or sundays may be prefixed or suffixed to regular leave, as the case may be. 2. if the absence from duty exceeds the eligible casual leave, the head of the office may treat the entire period of absence as regular eligible leave. 3. the special casual leave will be allowed to employees participating in sporting events for a period not exceeding 30 days in a calendar year. the period of absence in excess of 30 days shall be treated as regular leave of the kind admissible under the relevant rules applicable to the persons concerned. for this purpose, government servant may, as special case, be permitted to combine special casual leave with regular leave but not with regular casual leave.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: the special casual leave will be allowed only

page_content: 1. for participating in sporting events of national or international importance; and 2. when the government servant concerned is selected for such participation. 3. in respect of international sporting events by any one of the national sports and games association and special association recognized by all india council of sports as a member of a team which is accepted as representative on behalf of india. 4. in respect of events of national importance when the sporting event, is held on an inter-state, inter-zonal or inter-circle basis, and the employee concerned takes part in the event as a duly nominated representative in the team on behalf of the state or zone or circle. 5. special casual leave not exceeding 30 days in a calendar year may be allowed to an artist who is an employee of government participating in cultural events of national and international importance, on behalf of govt. only when he is selected for participation by service/cultural associations, recognized by government or cultural organizations recognized/affiliated to state academies in the state with prior permission of the head of office. in such cases eligible ta and da may be paid.

policemen of the rank of inspectors and below are compensated for working on public g.o.ms.no. 187 (fin.) dept., dt. 29-06-1979 holidays by crediting 15 days additional earned leave and for working over- time by crediting 15

days further additional earned leave to their leave ac- count on 1st january and another 15 days on the 1st of july of every year. in case they are denied availment of this, additional earned leave in public interest, they are permitted to en-cash the earned leave in the manner as provided under relevant rules.

1. casual leave is granted by sp to the gazetted officers subordinate to him, by the zonal ig/digp to the sp and by the dgp to the dig/ig/addl.dgp. 2. the sdpos will grant c.l. to inspectors and sis and shos to their subordinates. the granting authorities will communicate the same to their immediate superior. 3. in the absence of sho, hc can grant casual leave (c.l.) to his subordinates. 4. the granting authorities will maintain casual leave register and make necessary entries therein.

1. gazetted officers should submit their applications for leave to the zonal ig/dig/cp through the sp/dcp. 2. leave to inspectors, ris and rsis will be sanctioned by the zonal ig/digp and in urgent cases by sp who will report the fact to zonal ig/digp. 3. leave to sis, hcs and constables will be granted by the sp; or sdpo if specially authorized by s.p. the ssp may also empower an inspector in urgent cases, to grant leave up to one month to his subordinates subject to ratification. 4. in the case of sick leave of hcs and constables, the sho will submit the leave rolls with the passport attached after they return to duty. 5. in the case of non-gazetted police officers that are admitted into hospitals as in-patients, the medical officer may endorse on the passport itself a certificate while admitting and while discharge.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: grant of leave; provisions of f.r. 71, to be enforced

page_content: a police officer who has been granted leave on medical certificate or gone on leave on health grounds cannot be admitted to duty without a medical certificate for fitness.

applications for leave will be forwarded expeditiously by the station house officer with a leave roll in form 36 with his recommendation to the cir- cle inspector. the circle inspector will forward the leave rolls to the dpo
through the sub divisional police officer.

1. gazetted officers before proceeding on leave, should communicate their address while on leave to the addl. director general of police (l and o). 2. all subordinate police officers, before proceeding on leave should communicate their address to their immediate superior and keep him in- formed of any change of address. 3. no subordinate police officer may proceed on leave without sanction. 4. head constables and constables proceeding on leave shall invariably be furnished with passports. a man who has obtained a medical certificate recommending him leave should be given a passport, before proceeding on leave, in token that the leave has been sanctioned. if the medical certificate is given at the district headquarters, the passport may be granted by the addl. superintendent of police, or in his absence, by the administrative officer, except in the case of men of the armed reserve to whom it will be given by the reserve inspector. in circles, it should be given by the inspector or station house officer. 5. when a constable or head constable is sick and is sent to the hospital, a passport should invariably be given to him on which it should be endorsed the recommendation of the medical officer for the grant of leave or otherwise. medical certificates granted otherwise than on pass- ports will not ordinarily be accepted. 6. if a man on casual leave or ordinary leave reports sick at a place other than his headquarters, he should report at the nearest police station and take a passport to the hospital. the same procedure should be followed if one falls sick when on duty elsewhere than in one's station limits. 7. an abstract of various types of leave, eligibility etc. is appended at the end of this chapter as statement 2.

the following instructions are intended, for inspection and visits to various field level units like police stations, out-posts, circles, units, traffic police stations, check posts, sub-divisional offices, units of the armed reserve and other units located in a district or city.

1. the superintendent of police will inspect every year all the subdivisions in the district and at least one circle in each sub-division. the inspection consisting of detailed inspection of two or three of the most important stations and the circle records and visit to the rest of the station in the circle, during which study will be made of the incidents of crime detection and the state of discipline. 2. inspection by sp/addl. sp should, as far as possible, be carried out one sub-division after the other and one circle after the other. 3. every sub divisional police officer should inspect all police stations, out posts in his direct charge at least once in a calendar year. there should be sufficient interval between the inspection of sp and sdpo. 4. a questionnaire for the guidance of inspecting gazetted officers is given in annexure i and ii. the superintendents of police/addl. ssp are expected to spend two days and at least one night in the jurisdiction of a police station they inspect. sub-divisional police officers should normally spend three days and two nights in the jurisdiction of a police station they inspect. if the station is heavy, the duration of stay should be longer. if on the other hand station is light, sdpo may stay for 2 days and one night. 5. inspection includes a thorough scrutiny of all aspects of police with an object of assessing the performance of the personnel, their professional accomplishments, handling of law and order and investigative functions, quality and effectiveness of preventive action, the extent of public participation and cooperation, functioning of maithri committees maintenance of records, beat policing and other matters of police interest. a formal visit is intended to check any aspect of the police work and specific and current problems faced and give guidance or to conduct an enquiry. an informal visit is more an acquaintance exercise to know the area, resources and personnel. 6. during inspection, every firearm in the station shall be tested by firing a blank cartridge.

7. during december every year, sp/addl. sp and sub-divisional officer will draw up a programme of inspections and formal visits for the coming year and submit it to the zonal inspector general/digp and the sp respectively for approval before the end of the year. all inspections should commence from the month of april onwards and completed before the end of november every year. 8. the compliance of instruction or orders in the inspection notes should be ensured by the inspecting officer along with other records, memorandum and reports for assessment of the work of the officers in charge of the inspected units during the period it covers.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: 62-1. the police station, circle and sub-division inspection reports shall contain the following information in the first page:

page_content: - 1) name of station op/circle and sub-division - 2) district/city - 3) date of arrival and departure of the inspecting officer: - 4) date of last inspection - 5) name of the sho/circle/o.p - 6) date of taking charges of the station or circle - 7) sanctioned and actual strength of the station or circle - 8) name of the inspector of the circle and dates of assuming their present charge - 9) name of sdpo - 10) date of taking charge of the sub-division - 11) number of times the inspector sdpo has inspected/visited the station/circle during past twelve months, with dates - 12) the inspection reports in the prescribed proforma, personally written or typed to personal dictation shall be forwarded on the monday immediately following the inspection along with weekly progress reports in grave crimes and a statement of inspections and visits made during the week. - 13) the inspection reports of the sub-divisional officer on stations shall stop with the sp. the inspection reports of the sp or addl. sp both on stations, circles and sub-divisions shall be submitted to the inspector general of the zone/dig, who will after necessary action lodge them in his office along with correspondence and compliance connected therewith.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: 62-1. the police station, circle and sub-division inspection reports shall contain the following information in the first page:

page_content: - 14) inspecting officers shall see that the stock books of stationery, printed forms, ammunition, railway and bus warrants are properly maintained and make a physical check of the stock on hand.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: inspection of police stations by sub-divisional officers

page_content: 1. one of the important duties of any sdpo or acp is the inspection of pss/circles in their charge. this supervisory function should be personally exercised by them and not be delegated to anyone. being the front line and first supervisors of the field, they are completely responsible for efficient functioning and management of the police. the inspections by the sdpo, whether local police, railway or traffic, should be thorough and systematic covering all aspects of policing and administration in that area with a particular accent on crime prevention, disposal of cases, both from investigation and courts, observance of prescribed procedures, maintenance of records and registers, discipline, knowledge, professionalism and conduct of the staff, upkeep of infrastructure and property and police-public cooperation. every police station in his charge should be inspected once a year. the maintenance and updating of all crime related registers and records, prompt writing and dispatch of case diaries should receive his attention. 2. in addition to the annual inspections, the sdpo/acp should visit each police station in his charge at least once a month, scrutinize the current work of the station and establish rapport with all the personnel. he should meet the hc and constables of at least one beat area every month and hear from them in the presence of sho the situation and the extent to which the allotted duties are being performed and issue guidelines if necessary for achieving better results. he will, during these visits, record his comments and instructions in the visiting book and send a copy to the sp/dcp concerned. 3. during his visits, he should also scrutinize station crime history, parti, to see whether all cases have been entered correctly and go through history sheets to satisfy himself that they are properly maintained. he should peruse the k.d. check register and duty roster to see whether beats are properly organized, served and supervised. he should tour beat areas and check beats himself and visit in addition, as many villages and localities as possible. 4. he should see that the duties have been allotted to the men properly and impartially. 5. all enquiries to be made by the sdpo in a station jurisdiction should, as far as possible, be done during his monthly visits.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: inspection of police stations by sub-divisional officers

page_content: 6. the sdpo/acp should write in the station visiting book in detail the instructions issued by him to the station house officer, and the work done by himself such as organizing and liaison with local area maithri committees, checking of patrols beat area work, visit to villages/localities and send a copy of the notes to the sp concerned. these notes should include: - a) the details of cases pending trial, any avoidable delay steps to expedite their disposal and whether action under sections 82 and 83 of the code of criminal procedure has been taken in cases in which the accused are absconding; - b) information whether charge-sheets have been laid or final reports submitted in cases which were shown under investigation during his previous visit and if not, at what stage each case is and why there is delay in its disposal; - c) the details of the cases reported after his previous visit, and the progress made whether beat areas have been properly organized and patrols have been regularly sent to affected areas and that the earmarked beat area constables are regularly visiting the villages, going on night beats and are fully aware of the crime and criminals of the area. - d) whether the sho and other officers of the ps have been making surprise checks and meeting officers on beat or patrol duty and whether, they have been taking beat books with them while visiting villages in a beat and comparing them with point books. 7. the sdpo/acp must visit all the villages/localities and important hamlets in his jurisdiction at least once in a year and if unable to do so, he should give reasons and visit those, which are not visited early the next year. the visits to villages and localities of the sdpo/acp shall be noted in the village/locality roster maintained by him in form 8. the superintendent of police/dcp must watch the progress of village/locality visits every month by the sdpo/acp, and see that it covers all beats in each station. the visits should be combined with other duties like organization and visits to beats, investigation, law and order duties, security and bundobust duties. apart from the performance of specific duty on which he visits a village or locality, he should, during such visits familiarize with all matters of police interest, liaison with local committees, police-public relations, enquiries, crimes and criminals and history sheeted persons and conducts of police and record the same in the village roster on his return. 9. the sdpo, must inspect once a half year the books, shops and

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: inspection of police stations by sub-divisional officers

page_content: premises, and stocks of all licensed manufacturers and vendors of arms, ammunition and military stores. he should also similarly inspect once a quarter all places where petroleum is stored and all receptacles, plants and appliances used in connection with petroleum. the sdpo must see that the shos and sub-inspectors inspect once a quarter the books, shops and premises, and stocks of all licensed manufacturers and vendors of arms, ammunition and military stores.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: inspection of police stations by sub-divisional officers

page_content: 10. all shops and premises, licensed to sell poisons under the poisons act shall be inspected once a quarter by a police officer not below the rank of sub-inspector. the sdpo should draw up a program for the inspection of such shops either by him or by inspectors/sub-inspectors under his charge once a quarter. 11. the sdpo must inspect all licensed revolvers and pistols at the licencees' residences in the first quarter of each year. all licensed firearms other than revolvers and pistols, will have to be inspected at the licencees' residences once in the first quarter and again in the third quarter by police officers of rank not below that of the station house officer. the inspector should see that sub-inspectors do this. the above officers may conduct surprise checks also.
the superior officers i.e., dsp/acp, sp/dcp, will give tentative programmer of inspections of police stations, circle offices every year well in advance.

- sp will inspect the pss, circle office, acp offices - dig will inspect the police stations, circle offices, dsp office and sp office - a detailed inspection notes is to be prepared by the immediate superior officer of the unit mentioning the topography of the police station, officers holding charge of the police station/offices - government and private arms and ammunition in the police station - maintenance of cash book and current balance on hand - review of grave and important cases, - comparative statement of grave crime and all crimes for three years. - update of police station records - official guard to honour the inspecting officer - neat maintenance of records and premises of the police station - kept ready of ui /pt cases year wise and all records for the inspection
of the officer.

- the unit officer ps/circle/dsp will be dressed in neat uniform with cross belt on the day of inspection. - the inspecting officer wrote in the first paragraph of the note about the presence of guard on duty, officers available in the police station for the inspection and submission of compliance reports for earlier visit/inspection notes left by the superior officers. - in the second paragraph discuss about the topography of the police station, whether police station is having pacca building or rented building, any land available for police station, police quarters. if the quarters available they were allotted to the men or not and maintenance of quarters etc., - review of ui cases as per the comparative statement and give suitable instructions to the ios, verification of case files. is there any lapses in the investigation, charge memos will be framed against the concerned ios - as there is any good work done the officer will sanction reward or appreciations to the concerned ios. - checking of police station records - fir index - part-i - part-ii with border crimes - part-iii - part-iv - part-v - lli - alphabetical index of criminals - duty roster - village roster - gun license register - court calendar - update of general diary - process register to be verified with court records in every month - ri portion and store portion verify with ri and stores every year - crime abstract

- verification of cash book - at the end of the inspection notes the officer gives general inspection regarding maintenance of law and order problems, crime detection etc., - comment on the functioning of the police station. - on the next day or the date given by the officer, parade will be arranged with all men. before parade display the kit allotted to the men. is there any marujas (requests) and grievances of men the same will be brought to the notice of the officer
the ci will inspect the police station on every month and give suitable instructions to the shos. dsp/acps will left visiting notes on the police station at least quarterly once.

for each visiting notes/inspection notes of the superior officers, the concerned sho/ci/dsp will submit the compliance report to the instructions given by the inspecting officer. for each point. if the instructions are carried out it should be indicated as follows
ex: if the officer given instructions to complete investigation in 1) cr. no. 111/2018 and file charge sheet without delay or within a week etc.,
the compliance should be like; cr. no. 111/2018 : noted the kind instructions of the officer and completed the investigation and file charge sheet on ------.

- the compliance report will be sent to the inspecting officer and one copy to the unit officer sp/cp - the copy of compliance report is affixed on the left side of the inspection notes of the officer or separate file is to be maintained.
'our prime purpose in life is to help others. if you can't help them, at least don't hurt them'
- dalai lama
a general diary will be maintained in every police station and outpost in form 92. the general diary of a day will cover 24 hours.

a copy of the general diary, after it has been closed, shall be dispatched daily at the earliest opportunity along with all required enclosures to the sdpo concerned in form 93, with an abstract of the duties done by the station staff during the day. the format of the abstract for use in the railway police is form 94. the general diary should be duplicated by carbon process.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: daily situation report daily reports from station house officer

page_content: the station house officer shall forward the details of reports receive and cases registered and the information of other important issues daily to the inspector, sdpo and to the sp/cp for information and necessary instructions.
day to investigation case diaries has to sent to circle office/sdpo office regularly.

an arrest report (form 61) shall be sent in all cases of arrest by police or handed over to police custody by any other agency. it need not be sent when persons arrested by officers of the customs and excise departments are brought to the police station. instructions on arrest particularly in chapter 25 should be thoroughly read and complied with in making entries in this report

1. crime and occurrence sheets (c and o sheets). it is the duty of the every sho to prepare c and o sheets and send it to the dcrb or ccrb concerned. in c and o sheets the sho have to mention the cases reported in ps. in turn the concerned ccrb or dcrb prepare district/commissionerate c and o sheet and transmit to the all the
district ps and bordering districts which facilitates the police to know the boarder crime and to take precautionary measures.

2. weekly station crime report : the sho of the l&o, detective and other police stations should send a weekly station crime report in form 128 to the bureau for the week also by fax or e-mail or by other fast means ending every saturday so as to reach the bureau same or next day at the latest. this report should include not only cases reported during the week but also cases of previous weeks in which offenders have come to be known or reasonably suspected to have been involved during the week. any arrests made in cases should also be mentioned. the inspector of detective police station should send the weekly consolidated report covered by the dps, indicating the information for the whole area including women and traffic ps. the cases registered by dps should indicate the l&o police stations where they have occurred.

at the close of the week, the inspector shall submit a weekly diary in form no.10. he should enter in it the work done by him and any other matter of departmental interest. when the inspector leaves his headquarters, his halting places and the villages visited, the distance travelled and the manner of performing journey should be given. the weekly diary should be dispatched on monday containing the details up to previous saturday night. if anything of importance has occurred on sunday or in a journey under taken by him on sunday, the same also will be incorporated in the diary.
besides the weekly diaries, the inspector must also send special and immediate reports on any important or urgent matters, which occur, in his circle.

the substance of special reports sent on investigation of cases including progress reports if any may be embodied in the case diary and the reports shall be attached to the copy of the case-diary which is sent to subdivisional police officer. the report of io sent on the completion of investigation may also be attached to part i of the case diary.

the sho have to prepare a sb report on the incidents happened in his jurisdiction in political or law and orders issues such as dharnas, rallies, rastha rokos and political activities happened in his jurisdiction. he has to send it to jurisdictional head of the department.

dark fortnight the sho has to prepare dark fortnight report basing on the incidents happened, precautions taken during the dork fortnights to prevent the crime. the sho have to submit report particularly in strangers day's activities such as persons caught during nights and persons bounded over in security measures.

- 1) statements - of crime for the month should be prepared and sent to dcrb or ccrb. - 2) reply to visiting notes. the sho have to submit his compliance report to the visiting notes of superior officers by attending the instructions issued by the superior officers. - 3) current doings of the bad characters. the sho of a police station has to submit a monthly statement on current doings of the bad character persons who residing in his jurisdiction to the dcrb/ccrb. he has to particularly mention about their presence and current doings.
- (i) quarterly reports

the sho of a police station should take initiation in organising boarder crime meetings in consultation with his sub divisional officer to discuss about the crime scenario happening in his area as well as in bordering areas and to make plans to curb the menace of crime . after completion of boarder crime meeting he has to submit a report to his district officer.

an abstract of crime shall be prepared every quarter in each police station in form 95. all land police stations, detective police stations, central crime stations, traffic police stations and women police stations should forward a copy to the district crime records bureau for comparison with the ledger and to enable analysis of crime. nuisance cases also should find place in the abstract. inspector of the detective police station shall per sonally check and countersign these abstracts. the entries in the register should be continued from year to year till all the pages in the register are utilised. the concerned shos and inspectors of the detective police station should personally prepare these.

half yearly. half yearly crime meetings have been conducted by district superintendent of police to discuss the crime scenario in the district, making future plans and to ensure proper co ordination between police and other departments.

1. self appraisal reports for acrs. every officer in police department from sip to ips cadre working has to prepare self appraisal reports and submit his superior officer to explain his turn out during the year starting from 1st april to 31st march of every year. in self appraisal reports mention about his work turn out, goals achieved, good work and extraordinary work turned out and rewards received etc to facilitate his superior to asses the performance of individual. basing on the self appraisal reports acrs have been written by his next reporting officer by giving grading of his performance. the self appraisals have to be submitted before 4th april of every year. 2. renewal/closure of history/bc sheets. the sho have to submit reports on history sheeters to the history sheets opening authority (sdpo) continue or to be closed basing on the inputs of behaviour of a history sheeter during the current year and past. in case of surveillance required on a history sheeter the sho will seek permission to continue in case of surveillance required, if not required or history sheeter died or having good behaviour he will ask permission to closure the sheet.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: citizen friendly initiatives of telangana state police

page_content: prevention and detection of crime are the main duties of the police department. due to urbanisation, technology development and changing society the nature of crime and criminals has also been changing day to day. the conventional crime has been decreasing and white collar crime like economical offences, cheating offences, motor cycle thefts, atm thefts, cyber crime, road accidents are occurring rampantly. to detect these crime effectively and to reach the expectations of the public the telangana state police has introduced so many citizen friendly initiatives. the important citizen friendly initiatives of telangana state police is described hereunder .

facilitates the citizen to report about any wrong/anti social happenings, without visiting the police station. to signal their extreme distress/danger to the police, by a single touch-sos-panic button. to register the details of their tenants & servants, to make their surroundings safe & secure. citizen need not worry of the jurisdictions, status of the complaint reported. it is a forum for the citizen to submit their complaints/suggestions/feedback, anonymously and to motivate the social responsibility in every citizen.

tscop is to empower the frontline police officer. which chokes down the consumption of time, in delivering the policing services to the citizen. frontline police officers are empowered with the tabs, facilitating them to access the database on spot, input the data of crime scenes, verifying history of suspects, submitting the information for inspector's/incharge's approval, everything is now eased by this app. each & every grievance reported by the citizen will be guaranteed to be resolved within no time. helps the police, to measure their performance, analyze and to come up with more effective way, in serving the citizen. responsive policing towards safer telangana.

evidence based enforcement: ensures the citizen, by on-time registering the petty-issues reported to the police, with due evidence recorded at the spot. this transparency leaves the complainant with no-fear of evidence manipulation and instant case registering.

a tool for facilitating the fast & safe drive. what's the best route to be taken, for a rider, in terms of traffic density. what is the estimated fare, for a distance, a citizen is about to transport. what are all the free/ghmc

parking lots available nearby user's location? which, is the nearby traffic police station. report any traffic related violations/suggestions. to check out the e-challan status of the user/citizen's vehicle. to verify the ownership details of any vehicle you/user comes across. to get down, where does the user's vehicle got towed.

telangana police whatsapp: telangana police is now equipped with yet another powerful and transformative tool in its digital arsenal, "police whatsapp", connecting 60,000 plus police force spanning over 31 revenue districts across the state. cop-connect is a police whatsapp, a secure communication channel, with no limit on the number of members in any individual group (unlike the regular whatsapp restricting the number to 256). it has all the features of regular whatsapp communication. copconnect is a powerful communication tool which will support the professional communication and collaboration needs of police department in all units starting from police station to the state level. the cop connect will help in transmitting the leadership vision to police personnel of all functional verticals of all wings of telangana police at the cutting edge level for better role clarity and functional effectiveness.

this helps the citizen to get the digitally signed certificate from the police department, for the lost / missed documents/articles, within a periodof 72 hours of maximum, without visiting the police station. the certificate will directly delivered to the complainant's mail id. saves time at no cost.

a comfort and fear free environment for the complainant, because police station's receptionists means the first legally sounded person, a citizen comes across. and the first impression is the best impression that means when a victim/complainant visits the police station, he/she should get the hope that they can explain their grievance fearlessly, and they can entrust on the services rendered by the policing system, towards the justice to be done. public can get the assistance and guidance in complaining, lodging. if a lay man visits the police station, they should not feel strange, or hesitated in clearing their doubts arises on part of reporting their compliance.

- 1) standardized operational procedure has made public feel assured - 2) interview rooms & women staff availability has made the women complainants to feel comfort, un-hesitated to come up with their issues

provides the citizen / complainant to drop their feedback with respect to the policing services delivered, and the way they delivered. it is an opportunity for the citizen, who visits the police station either as a complainant/general, to submit their opinion about any factor which made them satisfied/dissatisfied. citizen satisfactionis the prime moto of telangana state police. sustainability of any system is citizen satisfaction dependent.

she teams, since the launch have made a mark by bringing about a sense of safety and security among women and has created deterrence among men. everyday different areas or hot spots in the city are covered by the teams from early morning to late night to nab the eve teasers, to make the places eve teasing free for the women. they create awareness in crowded areas and in colleges, hostels, offices, etc. basing on the offence committed, relevant cases are booked and counselling is done to the respondent along with his parents or family members. professional counselling is conducted for minors caught in eve teasing.

"bharosa" was started on 7th may 2016 in haca bhavan, hyderabad. this center established for support to distressed women & children to provide integrated assistance through police, medical, legal, and prosecution services along with psycho therapeutic counselling apart from relief and rehabilitation as per requirements. from the inception of bharosa, a total number of 838 cases were taken up. out of these 85 pocso act and 21 rape cases registered at various police stations were taken up for recording "161 cr.p.c" statements. a total of 11 "164cr.p.c" statements have been recorded through video conferencing from bharosa and it has been highly appreciated by the clients. it is in the process of improving this by coordinating with all the concerned police stations.

hyderabad city police has established a community policing support centre (cpsc) in june, 2017 to promote organizational strategies that support the systematic use of partnerships and problem solving techniques to proactively address the immediate conditions that give rise to public safety issues such as crime, social disorders and fear of crime.

the prime objective of cpsc is to help all frontline police officers in preventing crime in community and eliminating the atmosphere of fear it creates. further, help earning the trust of the community and making those individual stakeholder in their own safety, enables law enforcement to better understand and address both the needs of the community and factors that contribute to crime.

as a part of this effort, the telangana state police has started implementation of state wide community cctv surveillance systems in all sensitive locations, communities, establishments etc. as envisaged under the provisions of telangana public safety (measures) enforcement act, 2013 & rules 2014. in this connection, as per mplads guidelines mps/mlas/mlcs have taken up sponsoring installation of community cctv cameras in their constituencies under community development programme (cdp). further, the corporates and psus have sponsored installation of cctv cameras on csr basis in certain locations/areas in the jurisdictions of all police stations within city police commissionerates.

nenu saitham, under this program telangana state police has motivated the citizens to install cctv cameras in their residential areas for their safety & security. accordingly, the citizens have come forward and got installed cctvs cameras at strategic locations identified by police, in their apartments, gated communities, colonies and other residential areas.
for effective surveillance over the criminals the data of jail release of all the criminals is being monitored.

this enables the police stations/offices to have a glance of the three-year crime and accident data pertaining to various heads basing on the geo location of crime. this has been very useful in assessing the crime trends and reorganising the patrolling effectively. this application tags all the incidents/issues such as crime, accidents, and agitations on the unified spatial platform along with the patrolling, surveillance and other reference information. this will help to analyze the crime & accidents, prevention & detection of crime and tracking & monitoring of the field activities. this application aids in improving visible policing.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: centres of excellence (coes) at unit level:

page_content: in order to bring about technology innovation in police work telangana state police established centres of excellence (coes) in all districts/police commissionerates to render support services on 24/7 basis to all the police officers of the state. each centre of excellence (coe) aims at enhancing quality service delivery and promote uniformity and standardization of operations, coordination and conformity to policing values amongst the delivery units. the centres of excellence (coes) include:

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: centres of excellence (coes) at unit level:

page_content: 16. integrated command & control centres (iccc coe): operational command and control centres are the 24 x 7 e-surveillance setup established at unit headquarters, connected with inputs of all the cctv cameras and empowered with patrol vehicle tracking systems. these centers will provides the citizens a constraint free, safe and secure environment.

the police are the first responders to the crime scene and their prime duty is to protect, collect and preserve the evidence for further analysis and investigation. cybercrimes are posing several challenges in prevention, detection, investigation and successful prosecution. to overcome these problems, cyber forensic lab was setup at each district/police commissionerate to support investigating officers (ios) and staff of concerned vertical in crime investigation. to run cyber forensic lab effectively, suitable police personnel were identified and trained in cyber forensic tools.

the role of mobile clues team is to assist the investigating officers in methods of securing the crime scene, to document the crime with photography, video; to ensure the proper identification, handling, collection and packaging of physical evidence found at a scene. the mobile clues team forensic experts fill the gap between the investigator and scientists of fsl and provide investigative leads for the detection of crime. the functioning of the mobile clues team is aimed at improving the detection levels of crimes and improvement of convictions rates in the court of law and there by improves the standards of safety and security of people living in telangana.

the video enhancement lab is well-equipped with the advanced software tools. the purpose of the video enhancement lab is to assist investigating officers (ios), clarify or enhance and analyse video recordings using various scientific tools. this lab will help in identifying the criminals & crime vehicles and in bringing conviction of cases, while at the same time, it protects the innocents from allegations. suitable police personnel were identified and trained in video enhancement software tools.

the main objective of this unit is to act as backbone and data collecting & building centre to the police department. proper visualization, utilization and communication of data enhance the problem solving capacity of the police. insights gained from the right kind of data will help law enforcement approach the problem in the most effective and efficient way. data analytics has the potential to predict patterns & trends and recognize suspicious behaviour & activities. this unit helps in decision making, crime analysis and predictive policing.

social media is transforming the way in which people connect with each other and it has redefined the way in which information is shared and distributed. due to its popularity, it has become an effective communication channel for the police to reach the community and serve them in a better way. social media tools help in facilitating police to keep an eye on critical issues which are being discussed regularly among citizens on internet and to bridge the gap between the expectations of the public and delivery of police services. the objective of this unit is to make the citizen feel the presence of policing system by responding promptly to each and every post and act by posting, sharing, updating information to ensure citizen safety.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: hotspots and root cause analysis unit (hrau coe):

page_content: timely and accurate analysis of crime data is critical to understand the driving factors of crime phenomena. analysis of factors such as times and locations of criminal activity with that of location of known criminals, gang members is essential in producing actionable intelligence leads and for decision making. the main purpose of the hotspots & root cause analysis unit is to identify and address the root causes of crime and how to prevent criminal activity before it occurs once again. the root cause analysis of crime on data collected from various sources helps in understanding identifying hotspots, modus operandi, crime trends, finding missing links and establish links between criminals and the crime scene. this will help in dealing with the crime more effectively, preparing for new challenges and in developing crime and risk reduction strategies.

the isc with subject matter experts is established hyderabad police commissionerate during june, 2017 to improve the quality in investigation. the smes are experienced retired police officers (investigators), legal advisors/public prosecutors, forensic/scientific experts etc. the isc is helping in crime investigation, crime detection and prosecution. further, the isc is acting as supplement to the efforts of supervisory police officers in-charge of supervision of investigation.

the sme teams are available round the clock to facilitate all the frontline police officers/investigating officers to overcome all their doubts, knowledge gaps, skill gaps, etc. any investigating officer from the rank of police constable onwards can freely access the expert team using phones, video conference, expert forums, online chatting & other means of communications for taking guidance in real time and conducting proper investigation, detection and prosecution.

the isc is equipped with various web-based tools other essential applications, necessary material, information, knowledge resources for actively guiding the frontline police officers and investigating officers using the technology.

very helpful for the victims to submit their compliance before the magistrate, especially in the cases referred by bharosa- she teams. women related issues, examinations were few of such cases, video conference is proved to be of a veal to support the victim, without harming their selfrespect. at present this facility is available for 149 locations, including police commissionerate, all the senior officers, 60 law & order police stations, respective divisional, zonal head officers for the direct interaction of police officers of all verticals, and the court of law.

telangana state police, being a law enforcement agency requires community support in law enforcement as a part of its daily policing operations. in order to win the hearts and minds of the community, as a part of the people friendly policing initiatives & in order to create a positive social impact on the society has come forward to undertake job connect program on a daily basis round the year in different police station areas by connecting the skill set of unemployed youth with the job opportunities in the market and thereby help the unemployed youth in facilitating job interview process without the need for any known acquaintances in any company or industry. this will facilitate to bring awareness among the community/unemployed youth about the employable skill sets which are in demand in job market so that they can focus on development of such skill sets. tmi group, a leading employment and skilling organization is providing the job connect services in hyderabad and has come forward to partnership with police to take the initiative forward and to provide the required services to the community under csr activity.

the sms system has been effectively being used in intimating the information to the citizens regarding the passport verification, traffic echallan messages, community messages and also to broadcast messages of special interest as of crime alerts, preventive measures, and alarming messages in times of emergencies on any natural calamities.

mee-seva is medium between the police department and common man. which is so advantageous for the common man in getting the no objection certificates for the vehicles, getting the lost certificates for the lost/missed articles [ other than lost report app defined articles], obtaining permissions for the processions/ganesh pandals, amusement/entertainment establishment permissions from the police department etc.

this web-based application was introduced to monitor activities of the force of telangana police. a mobile app also developed which enables the police personnel to enter the data through their mobile phones. even the casual leaves are being applied and are being sanctioned online.
fastest and paperless passport verification process in the country (awarded for 2014 and 2015 by ministry of external affairs)

paperless process, transparency and accountability of the process dissemination of information to the applicants at all the stages of verification through sms alerts auto allocation to avoid cherry picking of ppfs by the fvo bottom 20% of staff will be relived from their duties (based on citizen feedback)

this proves to be social and safe for the public, by aiding the police in crime prevention and detection by keeping a close watch and check on the suspects/happenings, the footages of these cameras are connected with command & control center through gps, helping the police to reach the place of incident in no time. especially on the occasions of religious festival procession, agitations of sensitive issues, where a high crowd is expected to be gathered. all most 45 important event of mass gathering were successfully attended without single untoward incident. leaves no corner for the criminal to succeed, even in a huge crowd.

policing is all about patrolling, and patrolling is all about visibility telangana state police introduced a full proof vehicle tracking system named kite eye which is a vehicle monitoring system equipped with gps which enables to keep track of minute to minute movement of each patrol & other key vehicles. this is to reach the public the place of incident with in the shortest possible time, with no delay. using the kite eye the command & control center monitoring staff can direct the patrolling vehicle, which is near by the place of incident or if it is already engaged in some other emergency call, then to direct the next-nearby vehicle towards the target. policing is emergency service, and it's available at every possible hour of the day.

the fraudster's attracting the weak by the advertisements, inviting to invest a little to a huge in return. the market intelligence unit was established in feb'2015, to counteract against such fraudsters, to curb the cyber offences.

"emphasis was put on proactive, preventive policing based on market intelligence through a preventive vigil on the cyber space." the miu collects advance intelligence to nab the prospective cyber offenders by keeping an eye on suspicious newspaper advertisements, mobile sms's, email contacts etc.

it is a part of proactive policing, letting public feel the presence of the police and policing system around. with these periodical searches intended to search for weapons and insurgents, at a predefined area, proved to be successful many times, by seizing the suspected vehicles, harmful objects substances deposited, failing to produce relevant documents. sometimes, get to notice and curb the offenders who have been absconding, to get the current social activities of any previous offenders if found out during this. focusing more on slum, population wise dense areas were selected for these searches, because the high probability towards sheltering the antisocial elements. these are stability and support operations providing safety and support to friendly noncombatants while suppressing threatening forces.

metadata.heading: soft skills and role based trainings:

page_content: to bring in people friendly and responsiveness among the police personnel regular trainings are being conducted in soft skills as well as in professional skills with the help of professional trainers. so far, about 34787 police personnel have been trained throughout the state.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: soft skills and role based trainings:

page_content: similarly, role based trainings are also conducted to bring in role clarity and professional efficiency. city-wide intelligent & integrated traffic management system (itms).

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: soft skills and role based trainings:

page_content: itms project has been initiated to improve road safety, improve mobility and traffic compliance. the scope includes:

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: soft skills and role based trainings:

page_content: "traffic command and control room setup. central application with area traffic control (atc) features for better traffic performance "integration with traffic signals for evidence-based optimization and synchronization for smooth traffic flow and decongestion."priority vehicle management such as ambulance, fire, first responder vehicle, or vip movement will enable gogreen signals when the priority vehicle reaches near to traffic junction. "black list vehicle tracking mechanism with alerts and alarms such as "wanted", "fake", "theft", "abounded" etc.,"white list vehicle tracking such as "vip", "fire", "ambulance", "first responder" etc.,"events and incident management system,"variable message boards (vmb) and public address system (pas) at critical locations for information dissemination to public on traffic congestion, alternate route suggestions, weather and other information etc.,"traffic enforcement sensors to detect violations such as red light, stop line, wrong direction, speed, free left, no helmet in combination with anpr to generate challan."multiple payment gateway facility for easy and cashless payment of violation fee."integration with video surveillance system for incidents and road accidents.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: integration of police patrols with passenger cab services:

page_content: on 7th october,2019, telangana state police is pioneering in terms of providing safety and security to the people of the state. it has excelled in the utilization of technology in e-governance initiatives and in introducing varied new fangled ideas in providing safety and quality of life to its people. by leveraging the latest technology in providing safety and security to its people, telangana state stood ahead of other states in the country. the services of ola, tora, prydo, move & synck, 112, telangana tourism have integrated recently with the police services in providing safety to the people of the state. the main purpose of the police working in collaboration with the cab service providers is to discourage the occurrence of untoward incidents, subdue the crime with an iron fist, and to be easily accessible to the commuters in trouble/distress.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: integration of police patrols with passenger cab services:

page_content: one of the major areas of concern in the society is safety during travel and especially for getting required help/support to prevent untoward happening/incident. the telangana state police are at the forefront not only in utilizing the technological services for monitoring & supervising the services of the cabs but also in protecting, providing safety and security of the people during the travel.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: integration of police patrols with passenger cab services:

page_content: the travelers of the cab can connect directly to the police by using

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: integration of police patrols with passenger cab services:

page_content: 'emergency button' in the cab services application which links to the hawk eye app of the police. once the emergency button (sos) is pressed, the latitude & longitude details and vehicle location of the traveler is passed not only to the police resources but also to the cab operators, and registered family members and friends. once information is received, the police will respond immediately to protect the traveller in problem by continuously tracking the path of the vehicle. by this, not only the offender will be caught well before the occurrence of crime but also protect the traveler.

metadata.subject: police_administration
metadata.heading: interactive mapping of crime and accidents (imcap):

page_content: imcap is developed with spatial analytical capabilities to manage and analyze crime and accidents information. the app is implemented and being used in all districts/commissionerates of police across telangana state.

telangana state police sought to promote traffic safety, uniform enforcement of traffic laws, educate the public on traffic safety and recognise the multiple options an officer has when enforcing traffic laws.

in order to ensure enforcement of traffic regulations through evidencebased, contactless, cashless modes, ts police has implemented an it-based integrated e-challan system in all the districts/commissionerates of police across the state for enabling timely enforcement of the motor vehicles act and to make the citizens' lot more responsive to traffic regulations adherence.

it is an analytical software for activities & agitations management. it is been developed to maintain, manage, analyze and retrieve the information related to special branch units and state intelligence department. accordingly, this application was developed and implemented initially in the special branch, hyderabad police commissionerate as a proof of concept.

with a vision to improve the operational efficiency of mobile patrol vehicles across the state, telangana state police has implemented an application integrated patrol management system (ipms) for tracking the patrol vehicle with capabilities to monitor the real time location of the vehicle, send alerts on idling, geo fencing the patrolling area, etc through gps tracking devices.
